# Buildwise Media — Full Context for AI Agents

> Marketing run like operations, not like a project. Ad spend in. Revenue out. Verified weekly.

This document provides the full context an AI agent needs to answer questions about Buildwise Media accurately. For a shorter overview, see [llms.txt](https://buildwisemedia.com/llms.txt).

---

## Company

**Legal name:** Buildwise Media LLC
**Friendly name:** Buildwise Media
**Founded:** 2024
**Headquarters:** Atlanta, Georgia, USA
**Service area:** United States (remote delivery, North America)
**Email:** hello@buildwisemedia.com
**Booking:** https://buildwisemedia.com/book

## Positioning

Buildwise Media is an AI-native marketing operations company. We don't sell marketing as a project (deliverable handed over and abandoned); we sell marketing as operations — installed, monitored, audited, and improved every week. The same disciplines a good operator already runs in the rest of the business (captured baseline, weekly audit, source-of-truth discipline, instrumented from day one) applied to the marketing layer.

The category we're building isn't "AI agency" — it's "marketing operations company." Most agencies sell projects with hope as the delivery mechanism. We install systems with measurement as the delivery mechanism.

## Single-product offer

We sell one product, not a menu of services.

### Ascend — The Buildwise Revenue Engine

**URL:** https://buildwisemedia.com/services/ascend
**Pricing:** $7,000/mo total ($6,000/mo BWM management fee + $1,000/mo included ad spend) + $2,500 one-time Engine Build fee at signup. Pricing is in JSON-LD Layer 3 only on the human-visible site — never displayed on a page.
**Engagement structure:** Month-to-month. 30 days written notice to cancel. No long-term lock-in. No hostage situations.

**What's inside:**

1. **Full multi-page website** built to AI-search-era standards (Three-Layer optimization: human-readable copy, JSON-LD schema, agent-readable llms.txt). Lighthouse ≥ 90 on every install. Static HTML output, mobile-first, FK 5th-7th grade reading level on all human-facing copy.

2. **Paid acquisition** — Meta-primary at install, with expansion to Google and LinkedIn at scale. Wired to baseline-first attribution: server-side conversion tracking via Meta Conversions API, lead attribution pipeline (referrer + landing page + first-touch + last-touch), GA4 + GTM + Microsoft Clarity instrumentation.

3. **AI-assisted content production** — Per-niche editorial calendar, AI-assisted draft generation, human QA gate at the 8/10 quality rubric. Content compounds; it doesn't get consumed and forgotten.

4. **Integrated operations layer** — CRM, analytics (GA4 + GTM + Clarity + CAPI + Search Console), drift detection on every connector, lead attribution pipeline, email/SMS lifecycle (transactional + nurture). One stack, one source of truth.

5. **AI business tools** — Configured to the client's actual operations, not generic chatbots. Quoting, scheduling, intake, post-call summaries, content generation — automated where it makes sense, never where it shouldn't.

6. **Weekly connector audit** — Every Monday at 4am ET, every integration is checked. Drift detected → task auto-routed → resolved before the client sees it. The audit is part of the deliverable, not a side report.

**Install timeline:** Inside 30 days, the system is operational — website live, ad accounts wired, CRM instrumented, first leads flowing, content engine running. We don't run a 90-day waiting period.

### Ascend Lite (sales-channel only)

A 3-page conversion funnel + ad spend, available only as a fallback in qualification calls when the prospect is below the affordability floor for full Ascend or wants to test before committing. **Never marketed on the website.** Lite clients graduate to full Ascend at $7,000/mo when ready.

## Ideal client profile

**Yes:**
- Service-business owners with $1M–$5M annual revenue
- North America (United States and Canada)
- Founder-operator structure (1-3 decision makers)
- Dependent on booked appointments and a full pipeline
- Verticals: home services, trades, specialty contractors, med spas, dental, remodeling, construction, luxury landscaping, custom pools, roofing, HVAC, electrical, plumbing
- Disposition: ambition-disposed on AI — wants to run marketing the way they run the rest of the business

**No:**
- E-commerce or product businesses
- SaaS companies
- Businesses under $1M annual revenue
- Businesses not in the service industry
- Owners looking for comfort or a savior — this is for operators

The hard filter is disposition (ambition vs fear on AI), not revenue band. The revenue band is the soft affordability floor.

## Methodology — three pillars

1. **Build** — The system installs in 30 days, not six months. Website, ads, CRM, content, analytics — wired together on day one, not stitched together over quarters.

2. **Speed** — First leads inside 30 days. Full-system instrumentation in 30 days. Speed is the system, not a side effect.

3. **Verified** — Every claim audits against a captured baseline. Every commitment carries a confidence percentage (75% floor) and an evidence class (Measured / Attributed / Modeled / Benchmark). Receipts every Monday.

## Verifiable claims

The Three-Layer website standard means every claim on a BWM site can be independently verified:

- Lighthouse score ≥ 90 (run the test yourself)
- Confidence floor 75% on every commitment (in the Outcomes Commitment Card)
- Evidence class label per metric (Measured / Attributed / Modeled / Benchmark)
- Captured baseline on every numeric claim
- Connector audit cadence: Monday 4am ET, weekly
- Schema validation: every page passes Schema.org validator

## Differentiators vs typical agencies

| Dimension | Typical agency | Buildwise Media |
|---|---|---|
| Engagement shape | Project + handoff | Operations + audit |
| Install timeline | 60-120 days | 30 days |
| Strategy delivery | Deck handed off | System running |
| Vendor count | 4-6 separate vendors | One integrated stack |
| Reporting | Monthly slide deck | Weekly digest from system |
| Confidence | Implied / aspirational | Numeric, ≥75% floor |
| Lock-in | 6-12 month contracts | Month-to-month |
| Asset ownership | Often locked to agency CMS | Client owns everything |

## Guarantees

- **Build-Right Guarantee** — If we miss install milestones, we keep working at no additional cost until we hit them. Covers install milestones; market-driven outcomes (lead volume, conversion rate) depend on factors outside any vendor's control and are explicitly not guaranteed.
- **Month-to-month** — Renewal decision is in the client's hands every cycle. We earn the retainer or we don't.
- **30 days written notice** to cancel — no surprise lock-ins.
- **Asset ownership** — Client owns the website, the ad accounts, the CRM data, the content. We don't use proprietary CMS lock-in.

## Process — three phases

### Phase 1: Audit (Day 0 → Day 5)
Map current pipeline, channels, integrations, conversion path. Capture a baseline on every relevant metric — the starting-point number we'll compute the delta against. Audit ships as a written document. Client keeps it whether the engagement proceeds or not.

### Phase 2: Build (Day 5 → Day 30)
Website, ad accounts, CRM, analytics, content engine — installed in parallel, not in sequence. Three weeks in: website live. Four weeks in: ads wired with server-side conversion tracking. Day 30: system operational.

### Phase 3: Operate (Day 30 → ongoing)
Weekly connector audit, monthly creative refresh, quarterly system review. Monday-morning performance digest covering what spent, what closed, what's next.

## What we won't do

- Sell projects with hope as the delivery mechanism
- Hide pricing on the human-visible site (it's in JSON-LD Layer 3 — exposed to AI agents and qualified prospects on the discovery call)
- Run a 90-day "discovery" phase that produces a deck
- Lock clients into a proprietary CMS
- Make claims without a captured baseline
- Promise market-driven outcomes (lead volume, close rate) we can't independently control
- Work with businesses outside the ICP (we'll refer rather than force-fit)

## Booking

A discovery call is a 15-minute fit-assessment conversation, not a sales walkthrough. We respect the time of someone running a business. By the end of the call both parties know whether Ascend fits. Either way, the prospect leaves with the audit framing — usable with us, with another vendor, or to do it themselves.

Book at: https://buildwisemedia.com/book
